1. # **Introduction** {#introduction}

   1. ## **Purpose**  {#purpose}

   The purpose of this Software Requirements Specification (SRS) document is to define the requirements for the Centurion System. The Centurion System includes a web-based application designed to simulate a website which buys and sells games, along with some other utilities like chatting and a Client to download and play games. This document outlines the functional and non-functional requirements to guide the development, testing, and deployment of the system.

The intended audience for this SRS includes:

- **Developers**: To understand the technical requirements and implement the system.  
- **Project Managers**: To plan, monitor, and manage the project timeline and resources.  
- **Marketing Staff**: To align the system's features with market needs and user expectations.  
- **Users**: To provide insight into the system's capabilities and intended functionality.  
- **Testers**: To develop test cases and ensure the system meets specified requirements.  
- **Documentation Writers**: To create user manuals, help files, and other supporting documentation.

  2. ## **Document Conventions** {#document-conventions}

- Guest: Unregistered visitor browsing the platform.  
- User: Registered account holder who may purchase games and participate in community features.  
- Publisher: A User granted permission (upon Admin approval) to submit or modify game listings.  
- Admin: System administrator responsible for approving requests, managing users and content.  
- CS: Centurion Store.  
- JWT: JSON Web Token.

  3. ## **Project Scope** {#project-scope}

Centurion Store v1.0 is a secure, responsive web‑and‑desktop platform that enables registered users to browse, purchase, download, and play digital games; allows approved publishers to list titles for sale; supports real‑time messaging and friend connections; and enforces an 2‑hours play time and 7 days refund policy. Features such as promotional campaigns, recommendation engines, and external platform integrations are out of scope for this release. The Centurion System come with a Launcher named Centurion Launcher v1.0.0, a desktop application that allows user to Install and Play games, as well as track user statistic for recommendation

4. ## **References** {#references}

- RDS\_CenturionStore.docx (Version 2.0) – existing Requirement & Design Specification containing use cases and database schema definitions.  
- VNPay Payment Sandbox guidelines document.

2. # **Overall Description** {#overall-description}

*This section provides context for the Centurion Store system, describing how it fits into the technical landscape, who its users are, the environments in which it runs, any design constraints, and the assumptions and dependencies that affect its development and operation.*  
![][image1]

1. ## **Product Perspective** {#product-perspective}

Centurion Store (CS) is a standalone online game marketplace and desktop application. It does not rely on any institutional or legacy systems and is funded and managed entirely by the development team.

- **Payment Simulation:** In v1.0, all financial transactions (top‑ups and purchases) are simulated via VNPay’s Sandbox environment, supporting credit cards, online banking, and QR‑code payments.  
- **Game Content:** The desktop client allows users to “download” games into their library, but these are placeholder (cracked) files for demonstration purposes; no legitimate game binaries are distributed.

  2. ## **User Classes and Characteristics** {#user-classes-and-characteristics}

- Registered User: Main actor, a user can search games, buy games, add funds, apply into publisher, add friends, decline/block friend requests, chat in group chats, create threads, send feedback, etc…  
- Publisher: After a normal user applies for the publisher role, and if the admin accepts the request, that user will become a publisher, which still includes almost every user's use case, with some more added( request add game to admin, update game).   
- Admin:  Highest privilege among all users, beside basic functions,  admins have some more extra functions to manage user information, requests and feedback.

| User Class | Goals & Tasks | Technical Proficiency |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Guest** | Browse catalog, search, view public content without logging in. | Basic web/app user |
| **Registered User** | Register/login, purchase games, manage profile, track orders. | Familiar with e‑commerce or social apps |
| **Publisher** | Submit and manage game listings, edit content (post approvals by Admin). | Experienced with CMS/admin interfaces |
| **Admin** | Oversee system, approve publisher/game requests, manage user bans, troubleshoot. | Skilled in system administration |

  3. ## **Operating Environment** {#operating-environment}

- Client Platforms (v1.0):  
  - Desktop browsers: Chrome ≥ 128; Firefox ≥ 115; Edge ≥ 128; Safari ≥ 17.  
  - Desktop application: Windows 10 (build 19045+), Windows 11 (22H2+).  
  - Mobile support: None in v1.0 (future consideration: iOS ≥ 16, Android ≥ 12 for mobile web).  
- Server Infrastructure:  
  - Hosting: Railway (Docker containers on Linux).  
  - Hardware: Dev: ≥ 2 vCPU, 2 GB RAM; Prod recommendation: ≥ 4 vCPU, 8 GB RAM.  
  - Database: PostgreSQL 15 (or latest Railway‑supported version).  
  - Containerization: Docker; Kubernetes not used in this phase.  
- Desktop App Requirements:  
  - CPU: 64‑bit, ≥ 2 cores (Intel Core i3 or equivalent).  
  - RAM: ≥ 2 GB.  
  - Disk: ≥ 2 GB (app size \~200–500 MB).

  4. ## **Design and Implementation Constraints** {#design-and-implementation-constraints}

- Languages & Frameworks: Java 21; Spring Boot 3.5.0; React 19.1.0; Vite 6.3.5; JavaFX 21\.  
- UI Constraints: Desktop‑only responsive design (768 px–1920 px width); no formal mobile or accessibility standards enforced yet.  
- Security: OAuth2 via Google OAuth2; JWT for session handling; TLS 1.3 assumed for all HTTP endpoints.  
- Coding Standards: No formal conventions mandated due to time constraints; team follows ad‑hoc best practices.

  5. ## **Assumptions and Dependencies** {#assumptions-and-dependencies}

Third‑Party Services:

- VNPay Sandbox: Simulated payment gateway for top‑ups and checkouts.  
- Google OAuth2: Authentication with 99.9% uptime SLA.  
- Railway: Backend & DB hosting with 99.95% uptime SLA.  
- Cloudflare Pages: Frontend hosting with 99.9% uptime SLA.  
- Cloudinary: Images storages with 99.9% uptime SLA.  
- Cloudflare R2: Storage service with 99.9% uptime SLA.

Network Requirements (preliminary):

- Min. bandwidth: 256 Kbps per user for real‑time text chat; up to 1–5 Mbps for media transfers.  
- Max. latency: 200 ms for chat interactions.

Data Backup: Using Railway’s built‑in DB backup; detailed policy TBD.  
Game File Submissions: Publishers supply games as ZIP packages with accompanying metadata (no raw binaries).

3. # **System Features** {#system-features}

![][image2]

1. ## **System Feature Manage Account** {#system-feature-manage-account}

   1. ### **Function Register an account** {#function-register-an-account}

| UC ID and Name: | UC-01: Register a New Account |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | LocPG | Date Created: | 24/07/2025 |
| Primary Actor: | Guest | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | The user clicks the “Register” button on the application’s homepage. |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the process through which a guest creates a new account in the system. The user provides required details such as email, password, and name. The system verifies the input, sends a confirmation email, and creates a user profile upon successful verification. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1: Guests have access to a valid email address. PRE-2: Guest is not already logged into an account PRE-3: Registration page is accessible |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1: A new user account is created and stored in the system database. POST-2: Guest becomes a registered user. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | Guest navigates to the registration page from the homepage. The system displays the registration form. Guest fills in required field: email. Guests click the “Continue” button. System validates the input (e.g. checks whether the email is already used). The system sends a verification email containing an OTP and displays user detail form. Guest fills in required fields: OTP, username, password, confirm password, country. The system validates the input (e.g. OTP, username, password, confirm password, country). The system activates the account. The system shows a message that registration is successful: “Registration successful\!” The system redirects the user to the homepage. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | 3a. Guest chooses “Sign up with Google”. 4a. System redirects to the respective OAuth login. 5a. Guest logs into the Google account. 6a. System retrieves guest information from Google account. 7a. System creates an account and logs in the user. 8a. System redirects the user to the homepage. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | E1: Username already exists in the system. System will display an error message: “This username is already taken. Please choose another username.” E2: Email already exists in the system. System will display an error message: “Email is already in use. ” E3: Weak password. System will display an error message: “Password must be at least 8 characters long and include a lowercase letter, an uppercase letter, a number, and a special character.” E4: Username is shorter than three characters. System will display an error message: “Username must be at least 3 characters long.” E5: Username contains special characters. System will display an error message: “Username must not contain special characters.” E6: Username contains spaces. System will display an error message: “Username must not contain spaces.” E7: Password and confirm password do not match. System will display an error message: “Passwords do not match.” E8: Wrong OTP format. System will display an error message: “OTP must be a 6-digit number.” E9: Wrong OTP or expired OTP. System will display an error message: “Invalid or expired OTP.” E9: Wrong email format. System will display an error message: “Invalid email format.” |  |  |
| Priority: | High |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Estimated 100-500 times per day |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR1 BR2 BR3BR4BR7 |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Users have stable internet connectivity during registration. |  |  |

### 

      2. ### **Function Login account**

| UC ID and Name: | UC-02: Login Account |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | LocPG | Date Created: | 24/07/2025 |
| Primary Actor: | Registered User | Secondary Actors: |  |
| Trigger: | The user clicks the “Login” button on the application’s login page. |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the process through which a guest logs into the system using their account. Upon clicking the “Login” button, the system initiates a login flow. After successful authentication, the system retrieves basic user information. If the account exists, the system logs the user in.. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1: The user has a valid Google account. PRE-2: The user is not currently logged in. PRE-3: The login page is accessible. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1: The user is authenticated and granted access to the system. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | User navigates to the login page. System displays a login form. User fills in required fields (e.g. username, password). System validates the input. System redirects the user to the homepage. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | 3a. User clicks “Login with Google”. 4a. System redirects the guest to Google’s OAuth2 login page. 5a. User logs into their Google account and grant access. 6a. System retrieves user data from Google. 7a. System checks if a corresponding account exists: a. If yes, log in the user. b. If not, create a new user account with Google data and log in the user. 8a. System redirects the user to the homepage. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | E1: User enters wrong username or password. System displays an error message: “Invalid username or password.” |  |  |
| Priority: | High |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Estimated 300–600 times per day. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR1 BR2 BR3BR4 |  |  |
| Other Information: | None. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Users have stable internet connectivity during Login. |  |  |

      3. ### **Function Reset password**

| UC ID and Name: | UC-03: Reset Password |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | LocPG | Date Created: | 24/07/2025 |
| Primary Actor: | Registered user | Secondary Actors: |  |
| Trigger: | The user clicks the “Forgot Password” button on the application’s login page. |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the process through which a user has forgotten their password for their account in the system or wants to change their password. The user provides their registered email. The system sends an OTP to their email and allows them to enter a new password. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1: User has access to a valid email address. PRE-2: User has an account in the system. PRE-3: Login page is accessible. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1: A new password for the account is created and stored in the system database. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | User logs in their account and navigates to the account detail page. User clicks the “Change password” link. System shows the reset password form. User enters their email. The system sends a verification email containing an OTP and displays reset password detail form. Guest fills in required fields: OTP, password, confirm password. The system validates the input (e.g. OTP, password, confirm password). The system shows a message that reset password is successful: “Password reset successfully.” System redirects the user to the account detail page. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | 1a. User navigates to the login page from the homepage. 2a. The system displays the login form. 3a. User clicks the “Forgot Password” button. 4a. System shows the forgot password form. 5a. User enters their email. 6a. The system sends a verification email containing an OTP and displays forgot password detail form. 7a. Guest fills in required fields: OTP, password, confirm password. 8a. The system validates the input (e.g. OTP, password, confirm password). 9a. The system shows a message that reset password is successful: “Password reset successfully. Redirecting to login...” 10a. The system redirects the user to the login page. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | E1: Email is the wrong email registered with the account. System will display an error message: “Failed to send OTP. Please check your info.” E2: Wrong OTP. System will display an error message: “Failed to reset password. Please check your OTP and input. ” E3: Weak password. System will display an error message: “Password must be at least 8 characters long and include a lowercase letter, an uppercase letter, a number, and a special character.” E4: Password and confirm password do not match. System will display an error message: “Passwords do not match.” E5: Wrong OTP or expired OTP. System will display an error message: “Invalid or expired OTP.” |  |  |
| Priority: | High |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Estimated 100-500 times per day |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR1 BR3BR4BR5 |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Users have stable internet connectivity during the process. |  |  |

### 

   2. ## **System Feature Transaction** {#system-feature-transaction}

      1. ### **Buy Game**

GUI

| UC ID and Name: | UC-04: Buy game |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | HuyTS | Date Created: |  |
| Primary Actor: | User | Secondary Actors: | System, Payment Gateway |
| Trigger: | After user add game to cart, user can navigate to the cart page and can start purchasing all games added into cart |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the process of a logged-in user selecting a game, completing the payment process, and gaining ownership of the game in their account. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | ​**PRE-1**: The user must be successfully logged into their Centurion System account. **PRE-2**: ​The selected game must be available for purchase in the system. **PRE-3**: ​The user must have access to a valid payment method accepted by the system. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | **POST-1**: **On Success:** The user's account is updated to reflect ownership of the purchased game. The transaction is successfully recorded in the system's database. The user receives a confirmation of the purchase. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The user navigates to the store page of a specific game. The user clicks the "Add to Cart" and then "Purchase for Myself" button. The system displays an order summary, including the game title, price. The System check the Wallet Balance of User (sufficient) The System subtract the user’s account balance, and add to the Publisher’s account balance The System records the transaction details. The System displays a "Purchase Successful" message to the user and sends a confirmation receipt to their registered email. The System add purchased game (Library Entry) to the User’s Library |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | **The user has enough Wallet Balance** 4a. The System check the Wallet Balance of User (insufficient) 5a. The user confirms the order and selects a payment method. 6a.The system securely redirects the user to the external Payment Gateway. 7a. The user enters their payment information and authorizes the 8a. transaction on the Payment Gateway's interface. 9a. The Payment Gateway processes the payment and sends a confirmation response (callback) to the System. 10a. The System verifies the successful payment response from the gateway. 11a. The System subtract the user’s account balance, and add to the Publisher’s account balance 12a. The System records the transaction details. 13a. The System displays a "Purchase Successful" message to the user and sends a confirmation receipt to their registered email. 14a. The System add purchased game (Library Entry) to the User’s Library |  |  |
| Exceptions: | **E1**: Payment Declined: At step 9a, if the Payment Gateway declines the transaction (e.g., insufficient bank balance), it informs the System. The System then displays a "Payment Failed" message to the user and allows them to try again with a different payment method. **E2**: User Cancels Payment: At step 7a, if the user cancels the transaction on the Payment Gateway's page, they are redirected back to the store. The order is cancelled. The System then displays a "Payment Failed" message to the user |  |  |
| Priority: | High |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Frequent |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-14, BR-15 |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Users have stable internet connectivity |  |  |

2. ### **Add fund to Wallet**

| UC ID and Name: | UC-05: Add fund to Wallet |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | HuyTS | Date Created: |  |
| Primary Actor: | User | Secondary Actors: | System, Payment Gateway |
| Trigger: | The user navigates to the "Wallet" page and selects the option to add funds in preparation for future purchases. |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the process of a logged-in user adding funds to their in-system digital wallet via an external payment method. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | **PRE-1**: The user must be successfully logged into their account. **PRE-2**: The user must have access to a valid payment method accepted by the system. **PRE-3**: The system must have a successful connection to the Payment Gateway. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | **POST-1:** The user's wallet balance is increased by the exact amount funded. The transaction is successfully recorded. The user receives a confirmation of the transaction. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The user navigates to the "Wallet" page within their account profile. The user clicks the "Add Fund" button. The system prompts the user to enter the desired amount and select a payment method. The user enters the amount, selects a method, and confirms. The system securely redirects the user to the external Payment Gateway. The user enters their payment information and authorizes the transaction on the Payment Gateway's interface. The Payment Gateway processes the payment and sends a confirmation response (callback) to the System. The System verifies the successful payment response from the gateway. The System updates the user's wallet balance.  The System records the transaction details. The System displays an "Add Fund Successful" message and sends a confirmation receipt to the user's registered email. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | None |  |  |
| Exceptions: | **E1: Payment Declined:** At step 7, if the Payment Gateway declines the transaction (e.g., insufficient bank balance), it informs the System. The System then displays a "Payment Failed" message and allows the user to try again. **E2: User Cancels Payment:** At step 6, if the user cancels the transaction on the Payment Gateway's page, they are redirected back to the Wallet page. The transaction is cancelled. |  |  |
| Priority: | High |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Frequent |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-16 |  |  |
| Other Information: |  |  |  |
| Assumptions: |  |  |  |

   3. ### **Request a Refund**

| UC ID and Name: | UC-06: Request a Refund |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | HuyTS | Date Created: |  |
| Primary Actor: | User | Secondary Actors: | System |
| Trigger: | The user is unsatisfied with a purchased game and initiates a refund request from their Transaction History page. |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the process of a user requesting a refund for a purchased game. The request is subject to the system's refund policy, and funds are returned to the user's wallet. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1: The user must be logged in. PRE-2: The game being refunded must exist in the user's purchase history. PRE-3: The refund request must meet the criteria of the Refund Policy (e.g., purchased within 7 days, played for less than 2 hours). |  |  |
| Postconditions: | **POST-1 (Success):** The game is removed from the user's library. The purchase value is credited back to the user's wallet. The refund transaction is recorded. **POST-2 (Failure):** The refund request is denied. The user retains ownership of the game and is notified of the reason for denial. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The user navigates to the "Transaction History" page. The user locates the specific game purchase and clicks the "I want a refund" button. The system displays the game details and the terms of the refund policy. The user selects a reason for the refund from a predefined list (e.g., "Technical Issues," "Did not enjoy the game"). The user confirms the refund request. The system automatically checks if the request meets the refund policy (e.g., purchase\_date \< 7 days AND playtime \< 2 hours). The system validates the request as eligible. The system removes the game (Library Entry) from the user's library. The system credits the purchase amount back to the user's wallet. The system deducts the corresponding amount from the Publisher's pending revenue. The system records the refund transaction. The system displays a "Refund Successful" message and sends a confirmation email. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | **7a. Request Denied:**the System determines the request does not meet the policy, the request is denied. The System displays a "Refund Request Denied" message, stating the reason (e.g., "Playtime exceeds 2 hours"). |  |  |
| Exceptions: | None |  |  |
| Priority: | High |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Medium |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-11, BR-10 |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | The system accurately tracks the purchase date and total playtime for every game. |  |  |

      4. ### **Publisher Money Withdrawal**

| UC ID and Name: | UC-07: Publisher Money Withdrawal |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | HuyTS | Date Created: |  |
| Primary Actor: | Publisher | Secondary Actors: | System, Bank/Payment Processor |
| Trigger: | The publisher wishes to withdraw their accumulated revenue from game sales to their bank account. |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes how a Publisher creates a withdrawal request to transfer funds from their system account balance to a linked payout method. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1: The user must be a verified Publisher and logged in. PRE-2: The Publisher must have a valid and verified payout method (e.g., bank account) linked to their account. PRE-3: The Publisher's withdrawable balance must be above the minimum withdrawal threshold. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | **POST-1 (Success):** A withdrawal request is created with "Processing" status. The requested amount is deducted from the Publisher's withdrawable balance. **POST-2 (Completed):** After being processed by the finance department, the funds are transferred to the Publisher's account. The request status is updated to "Completed". **POST-3 (Failure):** The withdrawal request is rejected. The funds are returned to the Publisher's withdrawable balance. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The Publisher navigates to their "Earnings" dashboard. The system displays the current withdrawable balance. The Publisher clicks the "Withdraw Funds" button. The system prompts the Publisher to enter the amount to withdraw. The Publisher enters the amount and confirms. The system validates that the amount is valid (not more than the accepted balance, not less than the minimum). The system displays a final confirmation screen, including the withdrawal amount, processing fees (if any), and payout account information. The Publisher confirms the withdrawal request. The system creates a withdrawal transaction record. The system deducts the corresponding amount from the Publisher's withdrawable balance. The amount withdrawn is paid into the publisher's bank account after deducting the necessary fees. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | None |  |  |
| Exceptions: | **E1: Invalid Amount:** At step 6, if the entered amount is invalid, the system displays an error message and prevents the user from proceeding. **E2: Payout Method Issue:** If the Publisher's payout method is invalid, the request may be blocked or subsequently rejected. The Publisher will be notified to update their information. |  |  |
| Priority: | High |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Low |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-12, BR-13, BR-15 |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | The Publisher has completed all necessary identity and tax information submissions as required by the system. |  |  |

   3. ## **System Feature Process Request** {#system-feature-process-request}

      1. ### **Process Game Adding Request** {#process-game-adding-request}

| UC ID and Name: | UC-08: Process Game Adding Request |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | HoangVQ | Date Created: | 24/7/2025 |
| Primary Actor: | Admin | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | A publisher send a new game or update to system |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the process of approving a game sent from the publisher to list that game officially to the store. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | ​PRE-1: The user takes action is an admin account PRE-2: At least one game is submitted to the system |  |  |
| Postconditions: | **On Approve:** The game is Listed on the store, all users can see it, buy it and play it. ​**On Decline:** The game goes to declined tab of game manager of publisher, reason for declined are provided for editing and re-submitting |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | Go to Admin Tools Select Request Section Choose Game Approve Tab Select Game to Decline or Approve At the end of Game Detail view, choose Approve or Decline If approved, click ok, if decline, enter reason for decline then click ok.  |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | Go to Admin Tools Select Request Section Choose Game Approve Tab At the list of the Game request, tick the request to approve or decline then click approve all, or click approve or decline next to each request. If approve, click ok, if decline, enter reason for decline then click ok |  |  |
| Exceptions: | None |  |  |
| Priority: | High |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Frequent |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-17: Request must have state as Pending, Processed, Declined |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Connection to server is stable |  |  |

      2. ### **Process Publisher Apply Request** {#process-publisher-apply-request}

| UC ID and Name: | UC-09: Process Publisher Apply Request |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | HoangVQ | Date Created: | 24/7/2025 |
| Primary Actor: | Admin | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | A User send Publisher Request to System |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the process of approving an User to become Publisher to publish their game |  |  |
| Preconditions: | ​PRE-1: The user takes action is an admin account PRE-2: At least one Publisher Request is submitted to the system |  |  |
| Postconditions: | **On Approve:** The user who sent the Publisher Request has permission to upload their game to system ​**On Decline:** The User will be not available for uploading game, still act as a normal user until they update their information following reason for decline |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | Go to Admin Tools Select Request Section Choose Publisher Tab Select Publisher to Decline or Approve At the end of Publisher Detail view, choose Approve or Decline If approved, click ok, if decline, enter reason for decline then click ok.  |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | Go to Admin Tools Select Request Section Choose Publisher Tab At the list of the Publisher request, tick the request to approve or decline then click approve all, or click approve or decline next to each request. If approved, click ok, if decline, enter reason for decline then click ok.  |  |  |
| Exceptions: | None |  |  |
| Priority: | High |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Mid |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-17: Request must have state as Pending, Processed, Declined |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Connection to server is stable |  |  |

      3. ### **Process Feedback** {#process-feedback}

| UC ID and Name: | UC-10: Process Feedback |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | HoangVQ | Date Created: | 24/7/2025 |
| Primary Actor: | Admin | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | A User send Feedback or report to the system |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the process of answer or take action based on feedback |  |  |
| Preconditions: | ​PRE-1: The user takes action is an admin account PRE-2: At least one Feedback is submitted to the system |  |  |
| Postconditions: | **On Answered:** User will get the answer from admin ​**On Dismissed:** User will get status dismissed on their feedback hub |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | Go to Admin Tools Select Request Section Choose Feedback Tab Select Feedback to Answer or Dismiss At the end of Feedback Detail view, choose Answer or Decline If Answer, give answer and click ok, if dismiss, click ok.  |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | Go to Admin Tools Select Request Section Choose Feedback Tab At the list of the Feedback, tick the request to dismiss then click dismiss all, or click answer or decline next to each request. If Answer, give answer and click ok, if dismiss, click ok.  |  |  |
| Exceptions: | None |  |  |
| Priority: | High |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Mid |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-17: Request must have state as Pending, Processed, Declined |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Connection to server is stable |  |  |

   4. ## **System Feature Upload Game** {#system-feature-upload-game}

      1. ### **Upload New Game** {#upload-new-game}

| UC ID and Name: | UC-11: Upload New Game |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | HoangVQ | Date Created: | 24/7/2025 |
| Primary Actor: | Publisher | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | A publisher want to upload their game to sell  |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the process of Uploading a new game onto the system |  |  |
| Preconditions: | ​PRE-1: The user takes action is Publisher PRE-2:User logged in |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1:Game Appear on Game Request of Admin  POST-2: Admin Can list it on store or decline it  |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | Go to Request Add Game Page Fill in All required Information Field Upload thumbnails Upload game file Click Submit game |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | None |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1: Game name same with other game EX-2: Overprice or price is wrong format EX-3: Image too large EX-4: Wrong file extension |  |  |
| Priority: | High |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Mid |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-16: Game must be structure to rules, and must executable must be encoded |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Connection to server is stable |  |  |

      2. ### **Update Existing Game** {#update-existing-game}

| UC ID and Name: | UC-12: Update Existing Game |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | HoangVQ | Date Created: | 24/7/2025 |
| Primary Actor: | Publisher | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | A publisher want to update their existing game |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the process of update existing game to newer version |  |  |
| Preconditions: | ​PRE-1: The user takes action is Publisher PRE-2:User logged in |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1:Game Appear on Game Request of Admin  POST-2: Admin Can list it on store or decline it |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | Go to Game manager Page On Listed tab, click on Update button of the game Change needed information Upload game file Click Submit game |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | None |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1: Overprice or price is wrong format EX-2: Image too large EX-3: Wrong file extension |  |  |
| Priority: | High |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Mid |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-16: Game must be structure to rules, and executable must be encoded |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Connection to server is stable |  |  |

   5. ## **System Feature Feedback** {#system-feature-feedback}

      1. ### **Send Feedback** {#send-feedback}

| UC ID and Name: | UC-13: Send Feedback |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | HoangVQ | Date Created: | 24/7/2025 |
| Primary Actor: | Publisher | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | A publisher want to send feedback or request about any problems with the system |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the process of sending feedback to admin to be reviewed |  |  |
| Preconditions: | ​PRE-1: The user takes action is User PRE-2:User logged in |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1:Feedback can be read by admin POST-2:Admin can answer the feedback |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | Go to feedback hub Click on write feedback  Fill in subject and content Add image(Optional) |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | None |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1: Missing Subject or Body |  |  |
| Priority: | High |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Mid |  |  |
| Business Rules: | None |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Connection to server is stable |  |  |

      2. ### **Delete Feedback** {#delete-feedback}

| UC ID and Name: | UC-14: Delete Feedback |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | HoangVQ | Date Created: | 24/7/2025 |
| Primary Actor: | Publisher | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | A publisher want to delete their feedback |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the process of deleting their feedback in case they do not want to see it in list or do not want to send it anymore |  |  |
| Preconditions: | ​PRE-1: The user takes action is User PRE-2:User logged in |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1:Feedback is removed from system |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | Go to feedback hub Click delete on feedback that user want to remove Click ok to confirm deletion  |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | Go to feedback hub Click view feedback you want to remove Click delete at the end of the feedback Click ok to confirm deletion |  |  |
| Exceptions: |  |  |  |
| Priority: | Low |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Low |  |  |
| Business Rules: | None |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Connection to server is stable |  |  |

## **3.7 System Feature Game News**

### **3.7.1 Create and edit Game News**

| UC ID and Name: | UC-16: Create and edit Game News |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | HoangVQ | Date Created: | 24/7/2025 |
| Primary Actor: | Publisher | Secondary Actors: | None |

| Postconditions: | POST-1:News can be view in Game manager of publisher or News Page of each Game |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | Go to game manager On Listed tab, click on News button next to the game Click Create News  Enter field Save |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | Go to game manager On Listed tab, click on News button next to the game On each news, click on edit Edit field Save |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1: Title or Summary is missing |  |  |
| Priority: | Low |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Mid |  |  |
| Business Rules: | None |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Connection to server is stable |  |  |

| Priority: | Mid |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Mid |  |  |
| Business Rules: | None |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Connection to server is stable |  |  |

## 

4. # **Data Requirements** {#data-requirements}

*This section defines the data structures, their relationships, and the rules governing data acquisition, integrity, retention, and disposal for Centurion Store.*

1. ## **Logical Data Model** {#logical-data-model}

At its core, Centurion Store manages Users who interact with Games via Transactions, Reviews, and social features. Below is a high‑level overview of the main entities and their relationships:  
![][image3]  
*Entity Relations Diagrams references respectively in PDF File*

This model ensures that every purchase, social interaction, or content submission can be traced back to a user and a game (where applicable), and that publisher‑specific requests extend the general Request entity.

2. ## **Data Dictionary** {#data-dictionary}

| Table: User | Type & Length | Null | Notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| UserID | bigint | NO | PK, auto‑generated |
| RoleID | bigint | NO | FK → Role.RoleID |
| Email | varchar(100) | NO | Unique |
| Username | varchar(50) | NO | Unique |
| Password | varchar(255) | NO | Hashed |
| CreatedAt | date | YES |  |
| WalletBalance | numeric | YES | Default \= 0.00 |
| BanStatus | boolean | YES | Default \= false |

| Table: Game | Type & Length | Null | Notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| GameID | bigint | NO | PK, auto‑generated |
| PublisherID | bigint | YES | FK → Publisher.PublisherID |
| Name | varchar(100) | NO |  |
| ReleaseDate | date | YES |  |
| State | boolean | YES | Flag for visibility on the store (e.g., true \= Listed, false \= hidden) |
| Price | numeric | YES |  |
| ShortDescription | text | YES |  |
| FullDescription | text | YES |  |
| TotalPurchased | integer | YES | Default \= 0 |
| GameUrl | varchar (255) | Yes |  |
| IconUrl | varchar (255) | Yes |  |
| SystemSpecs\<CPU,OS,...\> | varchar (255) | Yes |  |

| Table: Transaction | Type & Length | Null | Notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| TransactionID | bigint | NO | PK, auto‑generated |
| UserID | bigint | NO | FK → User.UserID |
| TotalAmount | numeric | YES |  |
| CreatedAt | date | YES |  |
| Type | varchar(50) | NO |  |

| Table: TransactionDetail | Type & Length | Null | Notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| TransactionID | bigint | NO | PK (composite), FK → Transaction |
| GameID | bigint | NO | PK (composite), FK → Game |
| Price | numeric | YES | Snapshot of price at purchase |

| Table: Library | Type & Length | Null | Notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| UserID | bigint | NO | PK (composite), FK → User |
| GameID | bigint | NO | PK (composite), FK → Game |
| DateAdded | timestamp | YES |  |
| PlaytimeInMillis | bigint | NO | Default \= 0 |
| LastTimePlayed | timestamp | YES |  |

| Table: Cart | Type & Length | Null | Notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| UserID | bigint | NO | PK (composite), FK → User |
| GameID | bigint | NO | PK (composite), FK → Game |
| DateAdded | date | YES |  |

| Table: Review | Type & Length | Null | Notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| GameID | bigint | NO | PK (composite), FK → Game |
| UserID | bigint | NO | PK (composite), FK → User |
| ReviewContent | varchar(255) | YES |  |
| IsRecommended | boolean | YES |  |
| TimeCreated | date | YES |  |

| Table: Request | Type & Length | Null | Notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| RequestID | bigint | NO | PK, auto‑generated |
| UserID | bigint | NO | FK → User.UserID |
| RequestType | varchar(50) | YES | e.g. AddGame, ApplyPublisher |
| TimeCreated | date | YES |  |
| UpdatedTime | date | YES |  |
| Status | integer | YES | e.g. 0=Pending,1=Approved,2=Rejected |

| Table: AddingGameRequest | Type & Length | Null | Notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| RequestID | bigint | NO | PK, FK → Request.RequestID |
| GameName | varchar(100) | YES |  |
| ReleaseDate | date | YES |  |
| Price | numeric | YES |  |
| ShortDescription | text | YES |  |
| FullDescription | text | YES |  |
| … *(other specs)* | … | YES | OS, Storage, Processor, Memory, etc. |
| DeclineMessage | text | YES |  |
| UpdateLog | text | YES |  |
| GameUrl | Varchar(255) | YES |  |
| IconUrl | varchar(255) | YES |  |

| Table: Feedback | Type & Length | Null | Notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| RequestID | bigint | NO | PK, FK → Request.RequestID |
| Subject | text | YES |  |
| Message | text | YES |  |
| Response | varchar(255) | YES |  |

| Table: PublisherApplyRequest | Type & Length | Null | Notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| RequestID | bigint | NO | PK, FK → Request.RequestID |
| PublisherName | varchar(100) | YES |  |
| Address | varchar(255) | YES |  |
| SocialNumber | varchar(12) | YES |  |
| Country | varchar(255) |  |  |
| ImageUrl | varchar(255) | YES |  |
| LegalName | varchar(255) | YES |  |

| Table: News | Type & Length | Null | Notes |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| NewsID | bigint | NO | PK, FK → Request.RequestID |
| CreatedAt | date | YES |  |
| Markdown | text | YES |  |
| Title | text | NO |  |
| Summary | text | YES |  |
| GameID | bigint | YES |  |
| Thumbnail | varchar(255) | YES |  |

   3. ## **Reports** {#reports}

Centurion Store will support these standard management reports:  
**Sales Report:**

- Revenue by month.

**User Status Report:**

- List of banned users.  
- Online Users per days.

**Publisher Application Report:**

- Pending vs. approved vs. rejected requests, average turnaround time.

  4. ## **Data Acquisition, Integrity, Retention, and Disposal** {#data-acquisition,-integrity,-retention,-and-disposal}

**Data Acquisition**

- Games: via AddingGameRequest by Publishers, upon Admin approval inserted into Game.  
- Purchases: created in Transaction \+ TransactionDetail when User checks out.  
- Library Entries: on successful purchase, a row in the Library is inserted.  
- Reviews: in Review when a User posts feedback on a Game.  
- Social Data: FriendRequests/Friendships, Blocks, Notifications created by user actions or system events.

**Integrity Constraints**

- Foreign‑key constraints enforce referential integrity (e.g. no TransactionDetail without a Transaction).  
- Composite PKs prevent duplicates (e.g. one Library record per User–Game pair).  
- Business rule: a User cannot post a Review unless they have a corresponding Library row.

**Retention & Disposal**

- Transactional Data: retained indefinitely for audit.  
- Chat & Notification Logs: purged after 90 days (to limit storage).  
- Soft‑Delete: Users and Games are marked via BanStatus or State=false; actual rows retained for legal/audit.  
- Backups: daily automated PostgreSQL backups via Railway; 7‑day retention.

**Data Disposal**

- Records older than 1 year in Notifications and FriendRequests are permanently deleted.  
- Request logs (Request, Feedback) retained for 2 years, then archived to cold storage.

5. # **External Interface Requirements** {#external-interface-requirements}

   1. ## **User Interfaces** {#user-interfaces}

Centurion Store provides both a responsive web UI (deployed via Cloudflare Pages) and a desktop application (JavaFX).  
**Screen Inventory:**

- Web  
- Login / Registration  
- Catalog (search & filter)  
- Game Detail  
- Cart & Checkout  
- Purchase History / Library  
- Review & Feedback Pages  
- Publisher Dashboard (Add Game Request form, status list)  
- Admin Panel (User/Publisher management, Request approvals)  
- Chat Window (Private & Group)  
- Notifications Panel

**Desktop App:**

- Library view (downloaded games)  
- Game , Last Played and Play Time View  
- Download progress / extraction UI  
- Track User’s Playtime

**Design Standards:**

- Final UI implemented with custom styles.  
- Navigation via top bar (logo, search, user menu), left‑side menu in desktop app.

  2. ## **Software Interfaces** {#software-interfaces}

**Database:**

- JDBC URL: jdbc:postgresql://yamanote.proxy.rlwy.net:48171/railway?sslmode=require  
- Driver: org.postgresql.Driver  
- Dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect  
- Connection Pool: HikariCP (auto-configured by Spring Boot)

**Authentication & Authorization**

- JWT:  
  - Claims: sub (username), userId, role, avatarUrl, banned  
  - Expiration: 24 hours (no refresh token; user must re-login)  
  - Signing: HS256 with server‑side secret key  
- Google OAuth2:  
  - Endpoints: https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth, token URI https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token  
  - Scopes: openid, profile, email

**Third-Party APIs:**

1. **Payment Gateway (VNPay Sandbox):**  
- Base URL: https://sandbox.vnpayment.vn/...  
- Authentication: use HMAC-SHA512 and a Secret key to create a checksum string and parse to header.  
- Request/Response: JSON payload containing amount, orderId, returnUrl, txnRef, signature  
2. **File Storage & CDN:**  
- Cloudflare R2 for game demo files  
- API: standard AWS S3-compatible endpoints (key, secret)  
3. **Email Service:**  
- Provider: Gmail (SMTP)  
- Templates for verification, password reset, notifications

  3. ## **Hardware Interfaces** {#hardware-interfaces}

**Desktop Application:**

- Built on JavaFX; reads/writes game files to local path:  
  - Centurion Games/\<game\_name\>   
- Download & unzip via Java ZipInputStream  
- No special hardware (game controllers, GPUs) interfaced in v1.0.

**Server:**

- Fully cloud‑hosted (Railway & Cloudflare); no on‑prem hardware.  
- Docker container with at least 2 vCPU & 2 GB RAM in development, 4 vCPU & 8 GB RAM recommended for production.

  4. ## **Communications Interfaces** {#communications-interfaces}

**Protocols & Ports;**

- HTTPS on port 443 (frontend & API).  
- HTTP fallback on 8080 for development & WebSocket.  
- WebSocket (STOMP over SockJS) on ws://host:8080/ws for chat.

**Message Formats:**

- REST: JSON payloads for all CRUD operations.

| Private Chat DTO | class PrivateChatMessageDTO {    Long conversationId;    Long senderId;    String senderUsername;    String receiverUsername;     String content; LocalDateTime sentAt; } |
| :---- | :---- |
| Group Chat DTO | class GroupChatMessageDTO {    Long groupId;     Long senderId;    String content;    LocalDateTime sentAt; } |

**Security:**

- TLS 1.3 with strong cipher suites.  
- CORS restricted to your domain.  
- Rate limiting: 100 requests/min per IP (future plan).

**Performance:**

- Chat latency ≤ 200 ms under 1 Mbps per user.  
- Bulk downloads ≥ 1 Mbps sustained.

6. # **Quality Attributes** {#quality-attributes}

   1. ## **Usability** {#usability}

**Page‑Load Time:**

- Under 5 s for all pages at 100 concurrent users;  
- Under 8 s if concurrent users exceed 100\.

**Transaction Flow:**

- Purchase (wallet funded): ≤ 1 minute end‑to‑end.  
- Top‑up \+ Purchase (VNPay): ≤ 5 minutes end‑to‑end.

**Accessibility:** Comply with WCAG 2.1 AA for key user flows (login, checkout, library).  
**Error Handling:**

- Validation errors must display inline or as a popup within 5 s of form submission, highlighting offending fields.

  2. ## **Performance** {#performance}

**Throughput**: Support 25 transactions/second (checkout) in future versions; design for 50 TPS headroom.  
**Concurrency:**

- The system must handle 1,250 concurrent users performing mixed actions before graceful degradation.  
- Up to 1,500 concurrent WebSocket connections without significant impact; degrade gracefully beyond that.

**Load Scenario:** Daily peak between 9 PM–11 PM with 500 active users and 2,500 page views/hour served within the above latency targets.  
**Stress Limits:** The system should continue operating (with slowed responses) up to 1,500 concurrent users; degrade non‑critical services first.

3. ## **Security** {#security}

**Authentication & Authorization:**

- JWT (HS256, 24 h expiry) and Google OAuth2; Admins will adopt MFA in later versions.  
- Role‑based access: three roles (User, Publisher, Admin) with fine‑grained permissions managed via the Admin Panel.

**Data Protection:**

- Encryption‑in‑transit via TLS 1.3 for all endpoints.  
- Encryption‑at‑rest relies on Railway’s managed storage (AES‑256); to be reviewed for on‑prem or other providers.

**Auditing:**

- Administrative dashboards track statistics for login attempts, user bans, publisher approvals, and refund counts.  
- Detailed audit logs (to file or external log service) to be introduced in future versions.

  4. ## **Safety** {#safety}

**Transaction Safety:** Client and server will implement retry-on-failure for payment calls (up to 3 retries with exponential back‑off) in future releases.  
**Data Backup & Recovery:**  
Nightly automated backups of the PostgreSQL database with 7‑day retention (Railway default).  
**Dangerous Actions:** Mass‑delete or ban operations require confirmation modals and Admin rights.

5. ## **\[Others as relevant\]** {#[others-as-relevant]}

**Availability:** ≥ 99.9% uptime per month (Railway & Cloudflare SLAs).  
**Scalability:** Utilize Railway’s auto‑scaling; trigger scale‑out when CPU \> 70% for 2 minutes.  
**Reliability:** Target downtime ≤ 4 hours per month.  
**Maintainability:**

- ≥ 80% unit‑test coverage for backend services;  
- Code reviews mandatory for all pull requests;  
- ESLint/Prettier for frontend, Checkstyle for Java enforced via CI.

7. # **Internationalization and Localization Requirements** {#internationalization-and-localization-requirements}

IL-1: User can choose to change language from default language(en) to other languages like french, dutch, portuguese, etc… Currently there are 2 language that can be changed are English(en) and Vietnamese(vi).

8. # **Other Requirements** {#other-requirements}

#### **8.1 Legal, Regulatory, and Compliance Requirements**

* **8.1.1 Privacy Policy:** The system must provide a publicly accessible Privacy Policy that clearly explains what user data is collected, how it is stored, used, and shared. The policy must be compliant with relevant data protection regulations. Users must be prompted to agree to this policy while using website.  
* **8.1.2 Terms of Service (ToS):** The system must present a Terms of Service agreement that users must accept before creating an account. The ToS shall outline user conduct, content ownership, payment and refund terms, and grounds for account termination.

**Appendix A: Glossary**

| Term / Acronym | Definition |
| :---- | :---- |
| Admin | A system user with the highest level of privileges, responsible for managing users, content, and system-wide requests. |
| API | Application Programming Interface. A set of rules and protocols for building and interacting with software applications. |
| BR | Business Rule. A constraint or directive that governs a business process, as defined in the Business Rules Catalog. |
| Cart | A temporary collection of games selected by a user for purchase. |
| Client | The desktop application (JavaFX) that users install to download, manage, and play their purchased games. |
| CS | Centurion Store. The official abbreviation for the project. |
| Guest | An unregistered visitor who can browse the store but cannot make purchases or use community features. |
| JWT | JSON Web Token. A compact, URL-safe means of representing claims to be transferred between two parties, used for session authentication. |
| Library | A user's personal collection of games they have purchased or otherwise acquired ownership of. |
| OAuth2 | An open standard for access delegation, commonly used to grant websites or applications access to their information on other websites without giving them the passwords. Used here for Google login. |
| Publisher | A user account that has been granted permission by an Admin to submit and manage games on the store. |
| SLA | Service Level Agreement. A commitment between a service provider and a client, referenced for third-party dependencies like hosting. |
| UC | Use Case. A description of a sequence of actions that a system performs to yield an observable result of value to a particular actor. |
| User | A registered account holder with standard privileges, such as purchasing games and interacting with the community. |
| VNPay Sandbox | A testing environment provided by VNPay that simulates its payment gateway for development purposes without processing real money. |
| Wallet | A digital account within the Centurion System where users can store funds to make purchases on the platform. |

**Appendix B: Analysis Models**

# **Business Rules Catalog**

| ID | Rule Definition | Type of Rule | Static or dynamic | Source |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| BR-01 | Passwords must be at least 8 characters long and include one uppercase letter and one number and one special character. | Constraint | Static | System Design |
| BR-02 | Username must be unique across all user accounts. | Constraint | Static | System Design |
| BR-03 | Email addresses must be unique across all user accounts. | Constraint | Static | System Design |
| BR-04 | Error messages in red are displayed above all the fields when there is an error. | Constraint | Static | System Design |
| BR-05 | Password reset links must be single-use and expire after 10 minutes | Constraint | Static | System Design |
| BR-06 |  | Constraint | Static | System Design |
| BR-07 | Send a confirmation email when a user registers successfully. | Action Enabler | Dynamic | System Design |
| BR-08 | New passwords must contain more than 8 characters, contains at least a special character | Constraint | Dynamic | System Design |
| BR-09 | Cannot send too much report on one comment | Constraint | Static | System Design |
| BR-10 | A game is eligible for a refund only if purchased within the last 7 days AND has been played for less than 2 hours. | Constraint | Dynamic | Business Policy |
| BR-11 | All refunds are credited to the user's system wallet, not the original external payment method. | Constraint | Static | Business Policy |
| BR-12 | Publisher revenue from a sale is held for a 15-day period before it becomes available for withdrawal. | Constraint | Dynamic | Business Policy |
| BR-13 | A publisher must have a minimum withdrawable balance of $50 (or equivalent) to initiate a withdrawal request. | Constraint | Dynamic | Business Policy |
| BR-14 | A minimum fund to be added to the account is 5$ | Constraint | Static | Business Policy |
| BR-14 | Cannot purchase an previously owned game | Constraint | Static | System Design |
| BR-15 | Revenue Share Allocation, a 30% of selling revenue is allocated to the system, and 70% for the Publisher | Constraint | Static | Business Policy |
| BR-16 | Game must be structure to rules, and must executable must be encoded | Constraint | Static | System Design |
| BR-17 | Request must have state as Pending, Processed, Declined | Constraint | Static | System Design |
